new_documents.title: GST IMS
new_documents.summary: goods and services tax council secretariat government of india gst advisory no.: gstn/advisory/2026/08 date: march 18, 2026 subject: mandatory implementation of invoice management system (ims) for all gst filers — operational guidelines, reconciliation requirements, and penalty framework effective april 1, 2026 to: all gst registered taxpayers all tax deductors and collectors gst advisory no.: gstn/advisory/2026/08 date: march 18, 2026 subject: mandatory implementation of invoice management system (ims) for all gst filers — operational guidelines, reconciliation requirements, and penalty framework effective april 1, 2026 to: all gst registered taxpayers all tax deductors and collectors under gst all gst practitioners and chartered accountants all authorised service providers on gstn --- ntroduction the goods and services tax network (gstn) has developed the invoice management system (ims) to address the persistent problem of input tax credit (itc) mismatches between supplier filings (gstr-1) and recipient claims (gstr-3b). the ims creates a real-time invoice-level reconciliation dashboard on the gst portal that enables recipients to accept, reject, or keep invoices in pending status before itc is claimed. effective april 1, 2026, use of ims is mandatory for all gst-registered taxpayers with aggregate annual turnover exceeding rs. 5 crore in fy 2025-26. voluntary use is available for all other registered taxpayers from the same date. --- upplier action when a supplier saves or files their gstr-1 or gstr-1a, all b2b invoices are automatically pushed to the ims dashboard of the corresponding recipient. the recipient sees these invoices in real time before filing their gstr-3b. ecipient actions available for each invoice appearing in ims, the recipient can take one of three actions: a) accept — confirms the invoice is correct and itc will be claimed in gstr-3b b) reject — marks the invoice as incorrect; supplier must issue a credit note c) pending — defers the decision

supplier must issue a credit note c) pending — defers the decision
actions: Submit SOFTEX form for each export invoice to the bank within 30 days — required as Arvind Textiles is a goods exporter under FEMA
Renew LUT for FY 2026-27 on GST portal before March 31, 2026
Identify invoices above USD 25,000 where the revised 21-day/15-day SOFTEX timeline will apply from April 1, 2026
Engage CA firm for Annual FEMA Compliance Certificate by June 30, 2026
Verify if new USD 1 million threshold brings additional borrowings within ECB-2 reporting scope
drafts.penalty_if_missed: Not specified in circular — verify at source
drafts.email_body:
{
  "subject": "Compliance Advisory: GST IMS Circular for Arvind Textiles Pvt. Ltd.",
  "body": "Dear Arvind Textiles Pvt. Ltd.,

As a goods exporter under FEMA, Arvind Textiles Pvt. Ltd. is required to comply with the RBI's GST IMS circular to ensure timely submission of SOFTEX forms and renewal of LUT.

To ensure compliance, please take the following actions:

1. Submit SOFTEX form for each export invoice to the bank within 30 days from the date of export.
2. Renew LUT for FY 2026-27 on the GST portal before **March 31, 2026**.
3. Identify invoices above USD 25,000 where the revised 21-day/15-day SOFTEX timeline will apply from April 1, 2026.
4. Engage a CA firm for Annual FEMA Compliance Certificate by June 30, 2026.
5. Verify if new USD 1 million threshold brings additional borrowings within ECB-2 reporting scope.

Please note that the deadline for LUT renewal is **March 31, 2026**. Failure to comply may result in penalties, although the exact amount is not specified in the circular.

If you require assistance with any of these actions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,
Compliance Advisory Team

Source Reference:
Circular Title: GST IMS
Regulator: RBI
Circular No.: Refer official portal
Published Date: Recent
Source URL: Refer official regulatory portal"
}
drafts.internal_notes: CA notes — deadline was explicit, cross-checks needed for LUT renewal and SOFTEX submissions

actions: Renew Letter of Undertaking (LUT) for FY 2026-27 on GST portal before March 31, 2026

Submit SOFTEX form for each export invoice to the bank within 30 days — required as Kapoor Tech Solutions Pvt. Ltd. is a software exporter under FEMA with unrealised forex ₹5,800,000 and oldest invoice 112 days old
drafts.penalty_if_missed: Not specified in circular — verify at source
drafts.email_subject: Compliance Advisory: GST IMS Renewal and SOFTEX Form Submission
drafts.email_body:
Kapoor Tech Solutions Pvt. Ltd.,

This advisory is specifically applicable to your company as you are a software exporter under FEMA with unrealised forex ₹5,800,000 and oldest invoice 112 days old, necessitating the submission of SOFTEX forms to the bank.

As per the recent RBI Circular on GST IMS, we recommend the following actions be taken by your company:
1. Renew the Letter of Undertaking (LUT) for FY 2026-27 on the GST portal before March 31, 2026.
2. Submit the SOFTEX form for each export invoice to the bank within 30 days.

**Deadline: March 31, 2026**

Please note that failure to comply may result in penalties, although the exact penalty is not specified in the circular and should be verified at the source.

If you require assistance with these compliance requirements, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Sincerely,
Compliance Advisory Team

Source Reference:
Circular Title: GST IMS
Regulator: RBI
Source URL: Refer official regulatory portal
drafts.internal_notes: CA notes — deadline was explicit, cross-checks needed for related party transactions with Kapoor Tech Inc., Delaware USA

actions: Submit RBI monthly return (NBS-9) by the 7th of each month — required as RBI monthly return is due by 7th of each month
Settle pending demand of ₹42L under Section 271(1)(c) for AY2022-23 — penalty for concealment
drafts.penalty_if_missed: Not specified in circular — verify at source
drafts.email_subject: Compliance Advisory: RBI GST IMS Requirements for Sunrise Finserv NBFC Ltd.
drafts.email_body:
Sunrise Finserv NBFC Ltd.,

We have identified that your company is required to comply with the RBI GST IMS circular, as you are a Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).

To ensure compliance, please take the following actions by the deadline of 2026-04-07:
1. Submit RBI monthly return (NBS-9) by the 7th of each month.
2. Settle the pending demand of ₹42L under Section 271(1)(c) for AY2022-23.

Please note that the risk level for non-compliance is HIGH, and failure to meet the deadline may result in penalties.

If you require assistance with these actions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,
Compliance Advisory Team

Source Reference:
Circular Title: GST IMS
Regulator: RBI
Circular No.: Refer official portal
Published Date: Recent
Source URL: Refer official regulatory portal
drafts.internal_notes: [GUARDRAIL] This advisory was generated WITHOUT verified source documents (RAG confidence: low). CA must cross-verify all actions and deadlines against the original circular before approving. CA notes — deadline was estimated, cross-checks needed
